product_name: Visual Timetable for Children at Home
retailer: Amazon UK (sold by Create Visual Aids)
key_specs: {"piece_count":"35 picture cards, 2 A4 backing boards","dimensions":"Cards approx. 6 x 5.5 cm; boards A4","materials":"Water-resistant plastic cards, foamex boards, VELCRO fasteners","included_items":"35 home routine symbols, 2 display boards","other":"Shows up to 10 cards at once; UK-made brand"}
product_description_under_30_words: Thirty-five durable home-routine picture cards with two A4 boards and Velcro. Displays up to ten steps of the day at once.
ember_verdict: If fridge photos have not stuck, this is the grown-up version — rearrangeable home symbols for shoes, teeth, coat, bag. At nearly three, independence wants a visible plan, not a lecture. Try your own photos first; buy if DIY still fails after a fortnight.
rank_rationale: #1 over #2: When the whole day wobbles — not just one transition — a ten-step home timetable beats a morning-only board because nearly-threes need the full arc visible in one place.
why_it_fits: Mainstream UK home timetable with rearrangeable cards matching whole-routine independence without school-only symbols.
caveats: Generic symbols, not your child’s room — photos may still beat this. Ten-card display limit. Amazon primary because brand site bot-blocks automated checks.
gift_note: Parent-buy utility; not a gift unless parents asked.
ownership_note: Try phone photos of your real routine first — skip if that already works.
safety_notes: Small cards or tokens — supervise around younger siblings; store spares out of reach.
review_quality_note: Strong volume; sold by Create Visual Aids on Amazon UK.
preloved_signal_note: Laminated cards and metal boards survive second-hand; check Velcro and missing symbols.
evidence_notes: Primary Amazon URL smoke-checked HTTP 200 and buyable 2026-07-22. No under-36 choking exclusion found on live Amazon HTML. Ages 2+ taken from Create Visual Aids early-years / toddler home positioning (not bare Amazon Ages 3+ sidebar).
buying_factor_memo: Five factors set order for 34–36m visual routines. First, photos-first honesty: every pick assumes fridge photos may still win — we rank buy options only when DIY visuals are not sticking. Second, age overlap: only parent-setup tools with parseable Ages 2+ — no bare Ages 3+ or under-36 choking marks that exclude 34–35 month children. Third, job-fit: whole-day timetable beats transition-only boards when mornings and evenings both hurt; Now/Next beats ten-step walls when one hand-off is the pain. Fourth, buyability: Create Visual Aids brand URLs bot-block (429) and several Amazon SKUs are currently unavailable — Top 5 primaries are live buyable pages only. Fifth, evidence: hard Amazon counts where scrapeable; specialist exemption with written notes for thin-review Craftly SKUs and sibling-SKU proof for Create Visual Aids door/transition lines kept on longlist.

ember_verdict: Prettier than clinic boards — useful when mornings and bedtimes are the only war zones. Stars give a nearly-three a finished-job feeling fridge photos may lack. Try two photos on the bathroom door before spending.
rank_rationale: #2 over #3: When the pain is specifically morning and bedtime — not every transition — a star-progress AM/PM board beats a two-step Now/Next because the child sees the full wake-up or wind-down arc.
why_it_fits: Split AM/PM board with visible progress suits independence pride when whole-day charts feel like too much.
caveats: Only one on-page review; metal tokens need supervision around babies. Handmade lead time (Craftly ships Wed).
